title: How Facebook's Algorithmic Tweaks Affected Engagement with News URLs over Time
snippet: Media coverage and an academic session summary of Fabio Giglietto's presentation at the 2026 International Communication Association (ICA) conference in Cape Town. The post summarizes his research analyzing how Facebook's shifting algorithmic governance designs over the years have impacted audience reach, partisan alignment, and journalistic quality.
description: A detailed third-party blog post on snurb.info summarizing Professor Fabio Giglietto's research presentation at the ICA 2026 conference regarding Facebook's algorithmic impact on news URL engagement.

title: Indagine urbinate: "Così l’algoritmo influenza il voto" - Il Resto del Carlino
description: News article from Il Resto del Carlino

title: Algoritmi e democrazia, il caso Meta secondo lo studio di UniUrb
snippet: This news coverage discusses Fabio Giglietto’s study on Meta's political content reduction policy and its asymmetric impact on democratic processes. The article details how the research led to a formal request for clarification in the European Parliament and explains that the discovery of these algorithmic shifts was only possible due to transparency mechanisms under the EU's Digital Services Act.
description: An article discussing the political and public fallout of Professor Giglietto's research on Meta's algorithms, including its feature on Rai3's 'Report' and subsequent European Parliament inquiries.

title: Facebook, troppa visibilità a estremisti e complottisti: ecco come impatta Meta sulle elezioni
snippet: This article highlights research conducted by Fabio Giglietto examining the systemic biases built into Facebook's recommender systems. The coverage highlights how the platform's changes under-delivered on controlling political content, actually elevating extremist pages to become a primary information source on Facebook in 2024.
description: An article covering Professor Fabio Giglietto's research analyzing over 2.5 million Facebook posts, focusing on how political extremist pages bypassed Meta's filters to gain visibility during elections.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: This news article reports on a breakthrough study led by Professor Fabio Giglietto at the University of Urbino Carlo Bo [1.4.3]. The research, which analyzed over 2.5 million Facebook posts from 2021 to 2025, shows that Meta's political content reduction policy severely cut the reach of mainstream democratic politicians (by an average of 72%) while letting extremist and conspiracy-related content expand, provoking bipartisan political outrage and discussions of regulation in Italy.
description: A news article reporting on a landmark study led by Professor Fabio Giglietto analyzing how Facebook's algorithm policies reduced the reach of mainstream political figures while amplifying extremist content.
